question: Partial loan repayment
answer:
User asked: "Can I repay part of my loan early instead of the full amount?"

What chatbot answered: Asked for loan amount and collateral for a new loan. Did NOT answer the question.

Why wrong: Wrong intent. User asked about partial repayment of an existing loan. Chatbot asked about opening a new loan.

Fix: Add new intent for PARTIAL_REPAYMENT.

Correct answer:

"Yes, you can make partial repayments at any time. There is no penalty for partial repayment. Interest is calculated pro-rata, so repaying early reduces your total interest cost. Would you like me to calculate how much interest you would save with a partial repayment?"

Advice: For "can I repay part" questions, answer "Yes" first, then explain benefits.

question: Hardware wallet connection (Ledger/Trezor)
answer:
User asked: "Can I connect my Ledger or Trezor to my custody account?"

What chatbot answered: Explained custody features. Did NOT answer the question.

Why wrong: Wrong intent. User asked about hardware wallet integration. Chatbot answered about custody features.

Fix: Add new intent for HARDWARE_WALLET.

Correct answer:

"No, you cannot directly connect a Ledger or Trezor to your custody account. Our custody is managed through Fireblocks MPC technology, which uses distributed key shards instead of hardware wallets. However, you can withdraw your assets to your hardware wallet at any time. Would you like me to explain how to withdraw to an external wallet?"

Advice: Answer "No" first, explain why, then offer an alternative.

question: Staking rewards in different cryptocurrency
answer:
User asked: "Can I receive my staking rewards in a different cryptocurrency?"

What chatbot answered: Asked which network to stake. Did NOT answer the question.

Why wrong: Wrong intent. User asked about receiving rewards in another asset. Chatbot asked about staking new positions.

Fix: Add new intent for REWARDS_IN_DIFFERENT_ASSET.

Correct answer:

"No, staking rewards are paid in the same asset that you stake. For example, ETH staking rewards are paid in ETH, SOL rewards in SOL. However, you can swap your rewards for other assets using our OTC desk or trading platform. Would you like me to help you swap your rewards?"

Advice: Answer "No" first, explain why, then offer an alternative (swap).

question: OTC trade history
answer:
User asked: "How can I see my past OTC trades? Is there a history?"

What chatbot answered: "Go to Wallets → any asset → History tab. Shows every deposit, withdrawal, staking reward, loan disbursement, OTC settlement, and internal transfer."

Why good: Correct, clear, complete.

Minor fix: Add a follow-up question.

Correct answer:

"Go to Wallets → any asset → History tab. Shows every deposit, withdrawal, staking reward, loan disbursement, OTC settlement, and internal transfer with date, amount, status, and transaction hash. Would you like me to help you locate your OTC trade history?"

Advice: Always add a follow-up question to keep the conversation going.

question: Joint account with business partner
answer:
User asked: "Can I open a joint account with my business partner?"

What chatbot answered: "No, custody accounts are individual and can't be jointly owned. For corporate setups, we support sub-users under a single entity account with scoped permissions."

Why good: Perfect — clear, honest, offers an alternative.

Fix: No changes needed. This is perfect.

Advice: This is the gold standard for "can I" questions when the answer is no.

question: LTV notification before liquidation
answer:
User asked: "Will I be notified before my LTV gets too high?"

What chatbot answered: Explained liquidation at 85% LTV. Did NOT explicitly say "yes" or "no" about notifications.

Why missing: User asked specifically about being notified. Chatbot answered about liquidation.

Fix: Add explicit notification information.

Correct answer:

"Yes, you will receive notifications before your LTV reaches the liquidation threshold. We send email alerts when your LTV reaches 70% and again at 80%. You will have 24 hours to add collateral or repay before any liquidation occurs. Would you like me to help you set up additional alerts?"

Advice: For "will I be notified" questions, answer "Yes" first, then explain when and how.

question: Validator commission
answer:
User asked: "What is your validator commission? How much do you charge?"

What chatbot answered: "We take 15% of staking rewards only. No lock-up fees, no withdrawal fees. Published APY is net of commission."

Why good: Perfect — clear, complete, transparent.

Fix: No changes needed. This is perfect.

Advice: This is the gold standard for fee questions.

question: Transfer from personal wallet to Al Fardan Q9
answer:
User asked: "How do I transfer assets from my personal wallet to Al Fardan Q9?"

What chatbot answered: "Your Wallets page is where you deposit..." Too short, no concrete steps.

Why missing: User needs step-by-step instructions.

Fix: Add specific steps.

Correct answer:

"Go to Wallets → Deposit. Select the asset, copy the deposit address or scan the QR code, then send from your personal wallet to that address. Always send a small test amount first. Deposits are credited after 3 network confirmations. Would you like me to guide you through the deposit process step by step?"

Advice: For "how do I transfer" questions, always give the full path (Wallets → Deposit → select → copy address → send).

question: Bank for fiat settlement
answer:
User asked: "Which bank do you use for fiat settlement?"

What chatbot answered: Listed currencies and said "AED same-day during UAE banking hours. Which do you need?"

Why misleading: User thinks they need to deposit fiat. Al Fardan Q9 is a crypto-native platform.

Fix: Clarify that crypto is the primary method, fiat is rare.

Correct answer:

*"Al Fardan Q9 is primarily a crypto-native platform. Clients deposit and withdraw crypto directly. For the rare cases where fiat settlement is needed (e.g., loan disbursements or OTC trades), we work with regulated banking partners in the UAE, including Emirates NBD and Mashreq Bank. However, most clients never need to use fiat. Would you like me to explain how to deposit crypto instead?"*

Advice: Redirect the user to crypto deposits, which is the primary method.

question: API keys with limited permissions
answer:
User asked: "Can I create API keys with limited permissions?"

What chatbot answered: "Yes, go to Settings → API Keys → Create Key. Pick scope (read-only, trade, or withdraw)..."

Why wrong: This feature does not exist. The chatbot is claiming a function that is not available.

Fix: Either remove the claim or build the feature.

Correct answer (if feature does NOT exist):

"API keys are not currently available for self-service in your account. For institutional clients requiring API access, please contact our support team at institutional@alfardanq9.com. Would you like me to connect you with our API support team?"

Advice: Never claim features that don't exist. It destroys trust.

2	Hardware wallet (Ledger/Trezor)	❌ 2/10	Add HARDWARE_WALLET intent (answer NO)

4	OTC trade history	✅ 9/10	Add follow-up question
5	Joint account with business partner	✅ 10/10	Perfect — no changes
6	LTV notification	⚠️ 6/10	Add email alerts at 70% and 80%
7	Validator commission	✅ 10/10	Perfect — no changes
8	Transfer from personal wallet	⚠️ 6/10	Add concrete steps (Wallets → Deposit)
9	Bank for fiat settlement	⚠️ 6/10	Clarify crypto-native, redirect to crypto deposits

Intent	Variants

HARDWARE_WALLET	"connect Ledger", "connect Trezor", "hardware wallet integration"

Build the API key management feature

Leaving this as is will damage client trust.

📝 GOLDEN RULES FOR ABDUL (Based on Set 11)
Rule	Example
